text:
applies. Recently, this Court in Independent Sugar Corporation 
Ltd. v. Girish Sriram Juneja & Ors., 2025 SCC OnLine SC 181 
held as under:-
“49. Lord Atkinson in Corp. of the City of Victoria v. Bishop 
of Vancouver Island [1921 SCC OnLine PC 75] observed:
“In the construction of statutes, their words must be 
interpreted in their ordinary grammatical sense, unless 
there be something in the context, or in the object of the 
statute, in which they occur, or in the circumstances in which 
they are used, to show that they were used in a special 
sense different from their ordinary grammatical sense.”
50. That words in the statute are to be understood in 
their natural, ordinary and popular sense. This has been 
underscored by Justice Frankfurter, in the following opinion:
“After all legislation when not expressed in technical terms 
is addressed to common run of men and is therefore 
to be understood according to sense of the thing, as
